workflow.name: Compare product prices from Amazon, Walmart and Google Shopping via Telegram using OpenAI
workflow.description:
Send a product name to Telegram, compare offers across Amazon, Walmart, and Google Shopping, and get the best sensible buying recommendation with confidence level, price range, and a direct purchase link.

## What this template does

This template transforms Telegram into a smart product price comparison tool. Once a user sends a product name, the workflow simultaneously searches Amazon, Walmart, and Google Shopping, standardizes all results into a unified format, and delivers the most practical buying recommendation.

Rather than blindly picking the lowest price, an AI step filters out poor matches, suspiciously cheap listings, and mismatched product variants to ensure the recommendation is trustworthy.


## How it works

1. A user sends a product name to the Telegram bot.
2. The workflow queries Walmart, Amazon, and Google Shopping in parallel.
3. Each marketplace response is cleaned and mapped into a consistent offer structure.
4. All offers are merged and consolidated into a single dataset.
5. An AI agent evaluates price, seller, reviews, and match quality to identify the best sensible offer.
6. A Telegram message is sent back with the recommended price, seller, price range, offer count, confidence level, reasoning, and a direct purchase link.


## Requirements
Before using this template, connect the following credentials:

Telegram API
OpenAI
SerpApi

You'll also need to add your own SerpApi key, review the marketplace query settings, and adjust or remove any fixed search location values as needed.

## Example input

DJI Osmo Pocket 3 Creator Combo [file:73]

## Example output

🔎 DJI Osmo Pocket 3 (Creator Combo / Osmo Pocket 3 family)
✅ Best Price: $557.91
🏪 Seller: B&Z Legend
📊 Range: $6.85 - $1349.99
🧩 Comparable Offers: 69
🎯 Confidence: Medium
📝 Why this was chosen: Chosen the lowest sensible price among New/unspecified offers: B&Z Legend at $557.91. Much cheaper listings (< ~$200) appear anomalous or risky (very low prices, unclear sellers). There are lower open-box offers (e.g., $498.75) but they carry added risk; established retailers (Best Buy, Official DJI store, OE USA) list in the ~$569–$999 range if you prefer retailer security.
🔗 Link: https://www.walmart.com/ip/DJI-Osmo-Pocket-3-Creator-Combo-4K-120fps-Camera-1-CMOS-3-Axis-Stabilization-Face-Object-Tracking-Mic-Clear-Sound-Vlogging-Photography/10583721243?classType=VARIANT


If no strong match is found, the workflow prompts the user to try a more specific product name.

## Good to know

Results may vary depending on marketplace availability, search query quality, and regional settings. Using specific product names typically yields more accurate recommendations than broad or generic searches.

More specific product names usually produce better recommendations than broad searches.

parameters.content:
## 🛒 Find the Best Product Price Across Amazon, Walmart & Google Shopping

Send a product name to Telegram, compare offers across Amazon, Walmart, and Google Shopping, and get the best sensible buying recommendation with confidence level, price range, and a direct purchase link.

**What this template does**
This template turns Telegram into a smart product price comparison tool. When a user sends a product name, the workflow simultaneously queries Amazon, Walmart, and Google Shopping, standardizes all results into a unified format, and delivers the most practical buying recommendation — filtering out suspicious listings and weak matches using an AI step.

**How it works**
1. A user sends a product name to the Telegram bot
2. The workflow queries Walmart, Amazon, and Google Shopping in parallel
3. Each marketplace response is cleaned and mapped into a consistent offer structure
4. All offers are merged and consolidated into a single dataset
5. An AI agent evaluates price, seller, reviews, and match quality to identify the best sensible offer
6. A Telegram message is sent back with the recommended price, seller, price range, offer count, confidence level, reasoning, and a direct purchase link

**Requirements:** Telegram API · OpenAI · SerpApi — add your SerpApi key and review any fixed location/region values in the HTTP Request nodes before use.

**Good to know:** More specific product names yield more accurate results. If no strong match is found, the workflow prompts the user to refine their query.

## 📨 Input

User sends a product name via Telegram to trigger the workflow.

## 🔍 Marketplace Search

Searches Google Shopping, Amazon, and Walmart in parallel via SerpApi.

Each response is cleaned and normalized into a shared offer structure for consistent comparison.

⚙️ Add your **SerpApi** key to each HTTP Request node and adjust any fixed location or region values as needed.

## 🤖 AI Offer Comparison

Merges all marketplace results, then uses an AI agent (GPT-4) to evaluate price, seller reputation, reviews, and title match quality.

Avoids suspiciously cheap listings, weak matches, and clearly different variants — recommending the best sensible buying option.

## 📤 Output

Formats the AI recommendation and sends a Telegram message with: best price, seller, price range, offer count, confidence level, reasoning, and purchase link.

parameters.text:
=Act as a product price analyst.

Your task is to review all marketplace offers for the same product and suggest the best lowest price to buy.

Use only the input data provided.
Do not use external knowledge.
Do not guess missing values.

Input:
{{ JSON.stringify($json.data) }}

Rules:
1. Compare all offers by title, price, seller, reviews, and product URL.
2. Treat offers as comparable only if they refer to the same product family and close variant.
3. Exclude offers that are clearly different variants, such as different model, storage, Pro/Pro Max, carrier status, or bundle.
4. Exclude offers with unclear or invalid prices.
5. Mark condition from the title when possible, such as New, Refurbished, Restored, Used, Pre-Owned, Renewed, or Unknown.
6. Recommend the lowest sensible price to buy.
7. Do not automatically choose the absolute cheapest offer if it looks risky due to very low reviews, suspicious title, weak seller signal, or poor match quality.
8. If the cheapest offer is still a valid and sensible choice, recommend it.
9. Keep the reasoning short and practical.
10. Return valid JSON only.
11. No markdown, no code fences, no extra text.

Return this exact JSON schema:
{
  "product_name": "",
  "total_offers_reviewed": 0,
  "comparable_offer_count": 0,
  "min_price": null,
  "max_price": null,
  "avg_price": null,
  "median_price": null,
  "recommended_price": null,
  "recommended_seller": "",
  "recommended_url": "",
  "condition_summary": "",
  "confidence": "",
  "reasoning": ""
}
